title: W3C WAI: Captions/Subtitles
key_finding: Captions are synchronized text versions of speech and needed non-speech audio.

title: Section 508: Captions and Transcripts
key_finding: Speech above about 180 words per minute may be too fast for captions; keep captions short.

title: Szarkowska et al. 2024, PLOS ONE: sound-off subtitled video
key_finding: Sound-off subtitled viewing increased cognitive load and reduced comprehension, immersion and enjoyment; viewers processed subtitles more heavily.

title: Kruger et al.: Why subtitle speed matters
key_finding: Higher subtitle speed can reduce comprehension, increase incomplete reading and reduce rereading.

title: Mirzaei et al. 2017: Partial and Synchronized Captioning
key_finding: Partial synchronized captions produced full-caption-level comprehension while presenting less than 30% of transcript in an L2 context.

title: Kafle et al. 2019 ASSETS: Highlighting key words in captions
key_finding: Highlighted keywords were rated easier to read/follow and lower task load by DHH participants.

title: Schneider et al. 2018: Signaling meta-analysis
key_finding: Signaling benefits retention and transfer, affects eye fixations and reduces cognitive load.

title: Richter et al. 2016: text-picture signaling meta-analysis
key_finding: Cues that highlight organization of relevant information can improve retention/transfer.

title: Henderson & Hayes 2018: Meaning maps and attention
key_finding: Meaning maps predict attention in real-world scenes beyond low-level salience.

title: Schwetlick & Harrison 2024/2025 review: Visual Crowding
key_finding: Crowding impairs perception when objects/letters are surrounded by clutter.

title: Stewart et al. 2020: peripheral and foveal interactions
key_finding: Peripheral and foveal signals are integrated across saccades; detailed recognition depends on foveal/parafoveal sampling.

title: Luck & Vogel 2013: Visual working memory capacity
key_finding: Visual working memory is capacity-limited and varies across individuals.

title: Dias et al. 2016: Visibility of speech articulation
key_finding: Visual speech information can compensate when auditory speech is degraded.

title: Snell 2024: No, Bionic Reading does not work
key_finding: No significant reading-time benefit between Bionic and normal text.
supports: BCA as weak/optional; HBF negative control

title: Možina et al. 2025: Usability of Bionic Reading
key_finding: No clear speed/comprehension advantage; bionic formatting produced more saccades and longer reading time in some conditions.
supports: HBF negative control; BCA dose testing

title: Brown et al. 2015: Dynamic Subtitles user experience
key_finding: Dynamic subtitles produced gaze patterns closer to no-subtitle baseline than traditional subtitles in the study.

title: AIM 2024 / AViMoS video saliency prediction challenge
key_finding: AViMoS provides 1,500 videos with observer data, including vertical videos; saliency prediction is technically mature enough to inform placement.

title: Lee 2023: Learning benefits of moving text
key_finding: Moving text can help when aligned with logic, but excessive/inappropriate kinetic typography can cause fatigue and overload.

title: Caption Royale 2024: affective captions for DHH users
key_finding: Affective captions use typographic modulation to convey emotion; explored with DHH participants.

title: Gerber-Morón et al. 2018: subtitle line breaks
key_finding: Viewers preferred syntactically segmented line breaks; linguistic segmentation affects processing.

title: Duraj & Szarkowska 2025: non-standard TikTok subtitle styles
key_finding: TikTok subtitle style with emojis/non-standard typography affected preference and engagement in reported studies.

title: Alruwaili 2025: scroll immersion and short-form video use
key_finding: Short-form use and scroll immersion predicted attention difficulty, working-memory disruption and cognitive fatigue in survey data.

title: Fallon et al. 2024 Science Advances: Language at a glance
key_finding: The left temporal cortex detects rough syntactic structure starting as early as 125 ms after visual multiword expression onset.

title: Nielsen Norman Group 2024: A/B Testing 101
key_finding: A/B testing compares design variations with live audiences against predetermined success metrics.

title: NN/g 2026: Statistical significance is not practical significance
key_finding: Results must be reliable and worth acting on; statistical significance alone is insufficient.

title: Kumar et al. 2024: adaptive bandit experiments
key_finding: Bandit algorithms adapt assignment based on collected data and context.

title: Lakens 2024: benefits of preregistration and Registered Reports
key_finding: Registered Reports move peer review earlier so feedback happens before data collection.

title: Kishore et al. 2020: superiority, noninferiority, equivalence
key_finding: Non-inferiority/equivalence designs answer different questions from superiority designs and require margins.
supports: accessibility non-regression and visual recall guardrails

title: WCAG 2.2 contrast minimum understanding
key_finding: Sufficient contrast is needed so text can be read by people with moderately low vision or contrast impairment.
